philippines.title: Russian GM Ivan Popov to hold 15-board chess simul in Cebu
philippines.description:
CEBU CITY, Philippines — Renowned Russian Grandmaster (GM) Ivan Popov is set to treat Cebu’s chess community to a rare 15-board simultaneous exhibition match this month, part of the celebration of the 50th anniversary of Philippine-Russia diplomatic relations. The official launch of the event was held on Friday, May 8, at the Russian Center of the Philippine-Russian Business Assembly (PRBA) in Banilad, Cebu City. Honorary Consul for Russia Armi Lopez Garcia led the launch for the milestone celebration, joined by PRBA officers and key personalities from the local Russian community. READ: Trojans stay unbeaten at 12–0 after contrasting wins in […]...
Keep on reading: Russian GM Ivan Popov to hold 15-board chess simul in Cebu

philippines.title: CCSC rubberized oval repairs not part of city’s improvement plans
philippines.description:
CEBU CITY, Philippines — The planned upgrades to the Cebu City Sports Center (CCSC) will not include the rehabilitation of its worn-out rubberized track oval. This was confirmed by CCSC Manager Brando Velasquez in an interview with CDN Digital. On Thursday, Nestor Archival met with key stakeholders, including Velasquez and former Cebu City Councilor Joy […]...
Keep on reading: CCSC rubberized oval repairs not part of city’s improvement plans

philippines.title: Asean Maritime Center to be established in PH - Marcos
philippines.description:
CEBU, Philippines — President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. on Friday said all Association of Southeast Asian Nations (Asean) leaders unanimously agreed to establish an Asean Maritime Center in the Philippines. “I suppose the situation in the South China Sea becomes more unreadable. This becomes more important that there will be a central repository for maritime issues that will apply to Asean members,” he said as the 48th Asean Summit and Related Meetings concluded in the city of Lapu-Lapu in Cebu. Marcos, however, clarified that the proposed center was not meant to confront or push back on any country but to push […]...
Keep on reading: Asean Maritime Center to be established in PH - Marcos

energy.title: More Than 40 India-Bound Ships Remain Trapped Near Hormuz
energy.description: More than 40 India-bound vessels, nearly half of which carry energy products, are still trapped in the Persian Gulf, unable to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, officials told Indian outlet The Economic Times on Friday. The ships are laden with crude oil, liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), and LNG, as well as fertilizer and other products.    A total of 13 ships flagged to India are still stuck west of the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Indian government. The Indian authorities have drafted a list of 41 vessels for priority evacuation…

energy.title: A Golden Opportunity for Canada’s Oil and Gas Industry
energy.description: Canada has a “golden opportunity” to become a major global oil player as the war in the Middle East limits sources of crude and natural gas, the head of the International Energy Agency said, adding that “The cost of missing this train will be incredible,” as quoted by Bloomberg. Fatih Birol said the world was losing 14 million barrels in daily supply because of the war, and reliability has gained even more prominence for importers of energy commodities. usStocks.title: Alibaba Chip Smuggling Allegations Raise Fresh Questions For AI Growth Narrative
usStocks.description: US authorities are investigating a reported US$2.5b smuggling scheme involving advanced Nvidia chips shipped to China. Alibaba Group Holding (NYSE:BABA) has been cited as a possible end customer in the inquiry. The company has publicly denied any involvement or business relationship with the parties mentioned in the alleged scheme. Alibaba is a major Chinese technology group with businesses across e commerce, cloud computing, and digital services, so any link to restricted AI hardware...
